displayName: VAT Obligation Retrieved (SoR's word on the open period)
description: Notarize-only record of an open VAT obligation exactly as HMRC states it (GET /organisations/vat/{vrn}/obligations). This is the SoR's word on WHAT is due and by WHEN — it establishes the periodKey the return will be filed against and the dueDate that makes timeliness a signed fact (the later vat-penalty-observation binds HMRC's late-submission verdict against this dueDate). AGLedger does not compute obligations; it notarizes what HMRC returned. periodKey is HMRC's 4-char period identifier (may contain '#'); it is echoed into the validation gate and the filing receipt so the whole chain is provably about ONE obligation period.

vrn.description: The 9-digit VAT registration number the obligation belongs to.

periodKey.description: HMRC's period key for the obligation (up to 4 chars, may contain '#'). The join key for the whole filing chain.

dueDate.description: Statutory filing due date (HMRC 'due', YYYY-MM-DD). The timeliness baseline: a filing whose receipt processingDate is after this is late (HMRC adjudicates the penalty, not AGLedger).

obligationStatus.description: HMRC obligation status: O = open (not yet fulfilled), F = fulfilled. A return should only be filed against an O obligation.
